result: {"verdict":"accepted","guard_confirmed":true,"reviewed_sha":"b99637fe34eff5407a50f8f07bf0d2a6b67525ad","base_sha":"554d080839e1419c2242921e444d40d02c947815","comments":["docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:74 — r1 MEDIUM (need-completion vs fault rule) is closed coherently: the new constraint (:74-78), acceptance criterion (:119-125), §3 terminal-ledger paragraph (:229-240), §4.3 (:259-263), §4 closing (:272-276), and §5 (:280-286) state one non-contradictory rule — an unaccepted proposal settles unchanged and ends resize; after peer acceptance a live pipeline applies-or-faults while a closing pipeline satisfies the op through a terminal member ledger and normal END retirement (accepted ADD completes authentication and admits a member that immediately ENDs on the closed queue; accepted REMOVE is satisfied by the named member's already-completed END; need completion alone is never a SessionFault; an accepted op is never rewritten as refused). The race it governs is real at HEAD (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:189-196 retires workers when the queue closes and drains; crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1221-1225 cleanly finishes an orphaned late-ADD sink; crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:2062-2064 would then settle it accepted=true — the false settlement now outlawed), and the rule is implementable on the existing SinkControl::{Add,RetireOne} substrate (pipeline.rs:88-102) with ldt-1/ldt-3 guarding exactly these outcomes in both layouts.","docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:103 — r1 LOW (weak >8 growth proof) is closed: the deterministic guard now requires an advertised receiver ceiling of at least 17, identical epoch/op/target sequences through target 17 in both initiator layouts, and must turn red under production clamps at either 8 or 16 ('reaching 9 alone is not proof'); ldt-2 (:348-350) repeats the kill-both-clamp-mutations requirement. Realizable and sufficient: ceiling 17 ≤ DIAL_CEILING_MAX_STREAMS=32 (crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:46), and growth through 17 crosses both legacy caps — DIAL_FLOOR_MAX_STREAMS=8 (dial.rs:40) and the shape table's 16 tier (dial.rs:690-691).","docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:11 — goal fit verified against the repository; this is the direct restoration of settled D-2026-06-20-1/-2 (docs/DECISIONS.md:78) rather than a new design. Drift is real at HEAD: dial.rs:686-705 still maps 512MiB/10k files→8 with a 16 maximum tier, transfer_session/mod.rs:3572 rejects DataPlaneResizeOp::Remove ('tuner concern, future work'), and transfer_session/data_plane.rs:834 defers the live tuner. Everything the plan wires already exists: spawn_dial_tuner_with_resize (dial.rs:751), LiveProbe/SharedStreamProbes, DataPlaneSession::{connect,from_stream}_with_probe, both dial_source_data_plane/accept_source_data_plane constructors (transfer_session/data_plane.rs:860,985), DATA_PLANE_RESIZE_OP_REMOVE=2 on the wire (proto/blit.proto:183), receiver_stream_ceiling with 0=unknown clamped to the documented 32 default (dial.rs:89-94), and the floor min(DIAL_FLOOR_INITIAL_STREAMS, ceiling) already used at dial construction (dial.rs:303). One semantic-SOURCE controller with only SourceSockets::{Dial,Accept} role-specific, ADD+REMOVE from live telemetry within the receiver ceiling, deletion of every shape/8/16 authority, and identical-trace role guards satisfies each clause of the stated goal; the alternatives (shape table as a starting 'hint', destination or bilateral control, fresh policy design) violate the no-hard-coded-target goal, the telemetry-ownership constraint, or the measurement-first discipline respectively.","docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:337 — sequencing and residual-risk handling are sound: ldt-1 adds acknowledged membership while preserving current ADD behavior, ldt-2 deletes the shape authority in the same slice that activates the live controller (no dual-policy window) with the session contract updated in-slice, ldt-3 closes lifecycle/observer with mutation proofs and full gates, ldt-4 keeps hardware evidence a final non-gating slice with no in-slice constant tuning. The r1 accepted observation (blocked-ratio policy can boundedly ratchet toward the ceiling on source-bound transfers) remains handled as adjudicated: the policy freeze (:55-59), receiver-ceiling bound, complete decision-reason observation (:292-298), and ldt-4 trace recording expose the signature instead of silently regrading it; the plan correctly defers any policy change to a separately reviewed finding.","REVIEW.md:23 — status honesty is consistent across the range: the reviewloop row records 554d080 (r1 reopened) plus a78d553/41dcb4d (r2 pending); docs/STATE.md:5 keeps exact-8 narrowed to static-orientation parity with round 2 and owner Draft→Active still pending; DEVLOG.md:7-8 records the verdict-then-correction chronology one commit per finding; the r1 raw result, non-authoritative proxy error, and adjudication are retained verbatim under .review/results/. The diff is docs/records-only — no Rust/proto/runtime change rides along, matching the finding's scope.","docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:1 — independent guard proof executed in this worktree: on reviewed bytes, scripts/agent/check-docs.sh, git diff --check, and 35 semantic assertions (17 round-2-correction presences, 6 r1-defect absences, 12 architecture invariants) were all green; restoring only this file to base blob 46c2397 (554d080) turned exactly the 23 correction assertions red while all 12 architecture invariants stayed green, proving the r2 diff is the load-bearing fix for both reopened findings and that the guard discriminates precisely; git restore --source=b99637f returned blob b3add75 byte-identical to b99637f:docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md, all 35 assertions and both gates reran green, git status --porcelain is empty, and HEAD is b99637fe34eff5407a50f8f07bf0d2a6b67525ad. No endpoint, SSH, rig/benchmark, push, branch or worktree creation/deletion, artifact deletion, Time Machine, or mount state was touched."]}

comments: docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:74 — r1 MEDIUM (need-completion vs fault rule) is closed coherently: the new constraint (:74-78), acceptance criterion (:119-125), §3 terminal-ledger paragraph (:229-240), §4.3 (:259-263), §4 closing (:272-276), and §5 (:280-286) state one non-contradictory rule — an unaccepted proposal settles unchanged and ends resize; after peer acceptance a live pipeline applies-or-faults while a closing pipeline satisfies the op through a terminal member ledger and normal END retirement (accepted ADD completes authentication and admits a member that immediately ENDs on the closed queue; accepted REMOVE is satisfied by the named member's already-completed END; need completion alone is never a SessionFault; an accepted op is never rewritten as refused). The race it governs is real at HEAD (crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:189-196 retires workers when the queue closes and drains; crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1221-1225 cleanly finishes an orphaned late-ADD sink; crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:2062-2064 would then settle it accepted=true — the false settlement now outlawed), and the rule is implementable on the existing SinkControl::{Add,RetireOne} substrate (pipeline.rs:88-102) with ldt-1/ldt-3 guarding exactly these outcomes in both layouts.

docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:103 — r1 LOW (weak >8 growth proof) is closed: the deterministic guard now requires an advertised receiver ceiling of at least 17, identical epoch/op/target sequences through target 17 in both initiator layouts, and must turn red under production clamps at either 8 or 16 ('reaching 9 alone is not proof'); ldt-2 (:348-350) repeats the kill-both-clamp-mutations requirement. Realizable and sufficient: ceiling 17 ≤ DIAL_CEILING_MAX_STREAMS=32 (crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:46), and growth through 17 crosses both legacy caps — DIAL_FLOOR_MAX_STREAMS=8 (dial.rs:40) and the shape table's 16 tier (dial.rs:690-691).

docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:11 — goal fit verified against the repository; this is the direct restoration of settled D-2026-06-20-1/-2 (docs/DECISIONS.md:78) rather than a new design. Drift is real at HEAD: dial.rs:686-705 still maps 512MiB/10k files→8 with a 16 maximum tier, transfer_session/mod.rs:3572 rejects DataPlaneResizeOp::Remove ('tuner concern, future work'), and transfer_session/data_plane.rs:834 defers the live tuner. Everything the plan wires already exists: spawn_dial_tuner_with_resize (dial.rs:751), LiveProbe/SharedStreamProbes, DataPlaneSession::{connect,from_stream}_with_probe, both dial_source_data_plane/accept_source_data_plane constructors (transfer_session/data_plane.rs:860,985), DATA_PLANE_RESIZE_OP_REMOVE=2 on the wire (proto/blit.proto:183), receiver_stream_ceiling with 0=unknown clamped to the documented 32 default (dial.rs:89-94), and the floor min(DIAL_FLOOR_INITIAL_STREAMS, ceiling) already used at dial construction (dial.rs:303). One semantic-SOURCE controller with only SourceSockets::{Dial,Accept} role-specific, ADD+REMOVE from live telemetry within the receiver ceiling, deletion of every shape/8/16 authority, and identical-trace role guards satisfies each clause of the stated goal; the alternatives (shape table as a starting 'hint', destination or bilateral control, fresh policy design) violate the no-hard-coded-target goal, the telemetry-ownership constraint, or the measurement-first discipline respectively.

docs/plan/LIVE_DIAL_TUNING.md:337 — sequencing and residual-risk handling are sound: ldt-1 adds acknowledged membership while preserving current ADD behavior, ldt-2 deletes the shape authority in the same slice that activates the live controller (no dual-policy window) with the session contract updated in-slice, ldt-3 closes lifecycle/observer with mutation proofs and full gates, ldt-4 keeps hardware evidence a final non-gating slice with no in-slice constant tuning. The r1 accepted observation (blocked-ratio policy can boundedly ratchet toward the ceiling on source-bound transfers) remains handled as adjudicated: the policy freeze (:55-59), receiver-ceiling bound, complete decision-reason observation (:292-298), and ldt-4 trace recording expose the signature instead of silently regrading it; the plan correctly defers any policy change to a separately reviewed finding.

REVIEW.md:23 — status honesty is consistent across the range: the reviewloop row records 554d080 (r1 reopened) plus a78d553/41dcb4d (r2 pending); docs/STATE.md:5 keeps exact-8 narrowed to static-orientation parity with round 2 and owner Draft→Active still pending; DEVLOG.md:7-8 records the verdict-then-correction chronology one commit per finding; the r1 raw result, non-authoritative proxy error, and adjudication are retained verbatim under .review/results/. The diff is docs/records-only — no Rust/proto/runtime change rides along, matching the finding's scope.
